name: • Optimize ResponsiveModal to avoid remounting ▍ ▍ • Use
description:
• Optimize ResponsiveModal to avoid remounting
 ▍
 ▍ • Use React.memo where appropriate
 ▍
 ▍ • Lazy load heavy components
 ▍
 ▍ Now let me think about my unknowns:
 ▍
 ▍ • Is the infinite loop reproducible consistently? What triggers it?
 ▍
 ▍ • Does it happen on desktop, mobile, or both?
 ▍
 ▍ • The ResponsiveModal unmounts/remounts the entire Dialog/Drawer tree when media query changes - this is a known anti-pattern with Radix
 ▍
 ▍ • The form.watch() calls during render in CreateRoomContent create new subscriptions on each render
 ▍
 ▍ I should inspect a few more files: the Switch component source, the use-media-query hook, and the dialog/drawer components to understand the full picture.
 ▍
 ▍ Let me also check if there are any other modal usages or if this is specific to CreateRoomButton.
 ▍
 ▍ Actually, looking at the error more carefully:
 ▍ ```
 ▍ ../src/presence.tsx (159:15)
 ▍ setRef
 ▍ @radix-ui/react-compose-refs
 ▍ ```
 ▍
 ▍ This suggests the presence component from Radix is repeatedly calling setRef. In React 19, the ref behavior changed - refs can be callbacks that are called during render in some cases,
 ▍ and if a component re-renders repeatedly, refs get composed repeatedly.
 ▍
 ▍ Possible causes:
 ▍
 ▍ 1. The ResponsiveModal is switching between Dialog and Drawer, and each switch causes Presence to mount/unmount, triggering ref callbacks
 ▍
 ▍ 2. The SaveHistoryToggle onClick + Switch onCheckedChange interaction causes rapid state updates
 ▍
 ▍ 3. form.watch() during render causes re-renders
 ▍
 ▍ Wait, form.watch() in react-hook-form v7 returns the current value and subscribes to changes. When used during render (not in useEffect), it can cause issues. But more importantly, in
 ▍ CreateRoomContent, every render calls form.watch('maxUsers'), form.watch('maxTimeLimit'), form.watch('isTemporary'), form.watch('name'). This is actually fine for react-hook-form
 ▍ because watch reads from the current form state - BUT in React 19's strict mode or concurrent mode, this could be problematic.
 ▍
 ▍ Let me read the use-media-query hook and the dialog/drawer components.
                                                                                                                                                                                            
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
 ▍ ✓ read 1 file, searched for 1 pattern, listed 1 directory                                                                                                      (ctrl+o to expand) • 59ms 
────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

 ▍ Thinking: Let me look at the dialog, drawer, and switch components, and the use-media-query hook more closely. The use-media-query hook has a bug! It includes matches in the dependency
 ▍ array of useEffect, which means every time matches changes, it re-runs the effect, which could cause an infinite loop or at least unnecessary re-renders. More importantly, it uses
 ▍ window.addEventListener('resize', listener) instead of media.addEventListener('change', listener) which is the modern approach.
 ▍
 ▍ But the bigger issue is the ResponsiveModal - it conditionally renders either Dialog or Drawer. When isDesktop changes, the entire modal tree unmounts and remounts. This causes Radix
 ▍ Presence to enter/exit animations repeatedly.
 ▍
 ▍ Also, looking at CreateRoomContent:
 ▍
 ▍ • form.watch('maxUsers') inside NumberStepper
 ▍
 ▍ • form.watch('maxTimeLimit') inside NumberStepper
 ▍
 ▍ • form.watch('isTemporary') inside SaveHistoryToggle
 ▍
 ▍ • form.watch('name') inside Button2 disabled check
 ▍ 
 ▍ These watch() calls during render can cause issues, especially when combined with form.setValue that triggers re-renders.
 ▍ 
 ▍ And SaveHistoryToggle:
 ▍ • The outer div has onClick={() => !disabled && onCheckedChange(!checked)} 
 ▍ • Inside, Switch has onCheckedChange={onCheckedChange}
 ▍ • When the Switch is clicked, both the div's onClick and Switch's onCheckedChange fire, creating a double toggle (or rapid state changes)
 ▍ 
 ▍ Let me read the dialog, drawer, and switch components.
